name: Texas
description: The Lone Star State — BBQ, oil, the Alamo, and everything's bigger in Texas.

questions.q: What is the capital of Texas?

source.notes: Capital since 1839.

questions.q: What is Texas's official nickname?

source.notes: From the state flag's single white star.

questions.q: Which Texas city is the state's largest by population?

source.notes: About 2.3 million; fourth-largest U.S. city.

questions.q: What famous 1836 San Antonio battle is remembered with the phrase 'Remember the _____!'?

source.notes: Defended by fewer than 200 Texians against General Santa Anna's army.

questions.q: Which country borders Texas to the south across the Rio Grande?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed nonsensical 'Mexican country' wording. Texas shares over 1,200 miles of border with Mexico.

questions.q: Which only U.S. state is larger than Texas by total area?

source.notes: Alaska is roughly twice Texas's area.

questions.q: Which city in Texas calls itself the 'Live Music Capital of the World'?

source.notes: Home of South by Southwest (SXSW) and Austin City Limits.

questions.q: Texas was admitted to the U.S. as the 28th state in which year?

source.notes: Annexed December 29, 1845.

questions.q: Before joining the Union, Texas existed for nearly a decade as what?

source.notes: The Republic of Texas, 1836-1845.

questions.q: Which Texian general led the army to victory at the Battle of San Jacinto and later served as the Republic's first president?

source.notes: Also the city of Houston's namesake.

questions.q: What is Texas's official state flower?

source.notes: Adopted 1901.

questions.q: Which Texas city is home to the Space Center and served as the historic NASA Mission Control?

source.notes: 'Houston, we have a problem.'

questions.q: Which major river forms Texas's border with Mexico?

source.notes: Called Río Bravo del Norte in Mexico.

questions.q: Which other river forms much of Texas's border with Oklahoma?

source.notes: Fed by tributaries in the Texas Panhandle.

questions.q: Which Texas national park, in the state's southwest, features canyons, desert, and the Rio Grande?

source.notes: Named for the big bend of the Rio Grande.

questions.q: How many counties are there in Texas (the most of any state)?

source.notes: More counties than any other state.

questions.q: Texas's state motto is a single word. What is it?

source.notes: From the Caddo word 'teysha.'

questions.q: Texas is a major producer of which resource that flourished starting with the 1901 Spindletop gusher?

source.notes: Spindletop near Beaumont kicked off the Texas oil boom.

questions.q: Which NFL team plays in Arlington, Texas, and is nicknamed 'America's Team'?

source.notes: Plays at AT&T Stadium, Arlington.

questions.q: The San Jacinto Monument, which honors the 1836 battle for Texas independence, is noted for what record?

source.notes: 567 ft tall — just over the Washington Monument's 555 ft.

questions.q: Which U.S. president was born in Stonewall, Texas, and returned to his 'Ranch' after leaving office?

source.notes: LBJ's 'Texas White House' was in the Hill Country.

questions.q: On November 22, 1963, which U.S. president was assassinated in Dallas?

source.notes: Shot in Dealey Plaza; the spot is marked by an X on Elm Street.
